title: Commerce Department budget proposal revives concerns about TraCSS
summary: A budget proposal for the Department of Commerce has raised concerns about the future of the civil space traffic management system (TraCSS) currently under development. This could impact the progress and implementation of the system.

title: China’s Chang’e-7 arrives at spaceport for lunar south pole exploration mission
summary: China's Chang'e-7 lunar spacecraft has arrived at Wenchang spaceport for launch preparations. The mission is planned for liftoff in the second half of 2026 to explore the lunar south pole.

title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return
summary: NASA is preparing for the Artemis 2 mission's return, with the spacecraft scheduled to reenter on April 10 before splashing down off the California coast.

title: PLD Space secures $35 million European Investment Bank loan for MIURA 5 launcher
summary: PLD Space secured a $35 million loan from the European Investment Bank to fund the final development of its MIURA 5 lightweight rocket. The MIURA 5 is designed to deploy payloads of up to 1 ton to low Earth orbit.

title: Heather Pringle on the upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of a major space organization, discusses the upcoming Space Symposium and emphasizes the importance of future international collaboration in space during a SpaceNews podcast interview.

title: China conducts pair of Long March launches for Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellations
summary: China launched two Long March rockets (Long March 8 and Long March 6A) to deploy satellites for its Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellations. This marks another step in expanding the country's satellite networks.

title: A new kind of arsenal: commercial satellites
summary: The Pentagon is shifting its strategy to rely on commercial satellites for future conflicts, recognizing their growing importance in military operations. This move highlights the increasing integration of commercial space capabilities into defense planning.

title: What returning to lunar space means for human health
summary: Returning to the lunar surface poses significant health risks for astronauts due to cumulative and unpredictable effects of spaceflight. The upcoming missions aim to address these challenges after the last human lunar landing in 1972.

title: Portal Space Systems raises $50 million to accelerate spacecraft development
summary: Portal Space Systems, a space mobility company, has secured $50 million in funding to advance the development of its highly maneuverable spacecraft. This investment aims to accelerate the company's growth and technological progress in the space sector.

title: Space Force taps 14 firms for $1.8 billion GEO surveillance program
summary: The U.S. Space Force has selected 14 companies to compete for $1.8 billion in contracts to develop 'neighborhood watch' satellites for GEO surveillance. This program aims to enhance space domain awareness in geostationary orbit.

title: Artemis 2 spies a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon | Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026
summary: A new photo from the Artemis 2 mission captures Earth as a crescent above the moon's horizon. The image was featured as the Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026.

title: Ripples in spacetime may have revealed 1st evidence of tiny black holes born in the Big Bang
summary: Gravitational waves may have revealed the first evidence of tiny primordial black holes from the Big Bang, which could explain dark matter.

title: Watch NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth live online today (April 10)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are scheduled to return to Earth today (April 10) after a 10-day mission around the moon. Live coverage of their splashdown is available online.

title: Where will NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission land today?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission astronauts will splash down in the Pacific Ocean this evening (April 10). The chosen landing site was selected for specific operational and safety reasons.

summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon astronauts are scheduled to splash down today. The event includes multiple stages of reentry and landing.

summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts attended a private screening of 'Project Hail Mary' before their upcoming launch. They described the movie as uplifting and inspiring.

title: Crew of tiny worms readies for April 11 launch to International Space Station
summary: A crew of tiny worms is set to launch to the International Space Station on April 11 to study the effects of long-term spaceflight on human astronauts. The six-month mission aims to provide insights into human health in space.

title: 'It probably will start a precedent': Why satellite company's withholding of Iran imagery has this expert worried
summary: Planet Labs has decided to withhold satellite imagery of Iran and the broader Middle East conflict region indefinitely in response to a U.S. government request. An expert warns this could set a concerning precedent for future satellite data restrictions.

title: A house-size asteroid is heading toward Earth tonight: Here's what you need to know
summary: A house-size asteroid is approaching Earth tonight but poses no threat according to the European Space Agency's Risk List. The close approach will not impact Earth or the moon.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches follow the halfway point of NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: NASASpaceFlight.com reports that the Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The news references the historic Apollo 17 mission as a comparison point.

summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz from various locations including Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory America is involved in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in its partnership with the agency for crewed lunar missions. This collaboration represents a full-circle moment for the company as it contributes to NASA's return to deep space exploration.

summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at ESA's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or efficiency in directional control systems. This innovation could enhance mission accuracy and operational capabilities in space.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials with greater efficiency in space-like conditions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating microgravity conditions demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: Researchers have developed a silicon chip capable of performing multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space-based computing. This advancement could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo announced a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to enhance power capabilities for this upcoming flight.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog has supplied over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company highlights its critical role in the flight's success.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: Artemis II's Orion spacecraft features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space, marking a significant step for European spaceflight contributions. This collaboration highlights Airbus's role in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investment from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch, Brian Rogers, highlights the competitive pressures in the 2026 launch industry, noting that missed deadlines can eliminate contenders. The 'Golden Dome' project exemplifies how capability gates can determine winners and losers in space access.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. The announcement highlights the company's focus on advancing its satellite technology.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. It highlights the evolving nature of ISAM as a field within the space industry.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-developed radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. HERA will act as an early warning system for solar events.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are preparing to oppose the Trump administration's proposed $18.8 billion NASA budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. This marks a recurring conflict over NASA's funding priorities.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with co-leads from Activate Capital and Shield Capital.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to push data from centralized nodes to the tactical edge for faster decision-making in conflicts. Network integration remains a key challenge in this effort.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor launched two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisits. This innovation enhances commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in firm fixed-price contracts under the 'Andromeda' program for Geosynchronous Space Domain Awareness. The contracts aim to enhance tracking and monitoring capabilities in Earth's geosynchronous orbit.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG) appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its second CEO, replacing Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to accelerate the development of maneuverable spacecraft and expand its manufacturing capacity. The startup is transitioning into production mode as it grows its team.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. The company's President & CEO, Eiichi Yonekura, discussed these plans in a recent interview.

title: FCC to Vote on Order to Update EPFD Framework
summary: The FCC is scheduled to vote on updating satellite spectrum-sharing rules between GEO and NGSO systems later this month. The proposed changes aim to replace the existing EPFD framework.

title: Italy and Argentina to Enhance Space Collaboration With Telespazio and VENG Deal
summary: Italy and Argentina have agreed to enhance their space collaboration through a new deal between e-GEOS (a joint venture of the Italian Space Agency and Telespazio) and VENG. The multi-year cooperation focuses on space data applications.

title: Northrop Grumman to Build HUGEO Satellite for Hungary’s 4iG
summary: Northrop Grumman has signed a deal with Hungary's 4iG Space and Defense Technologies to build the nation's first communications satellite, named HUGEO. The agreement marks a significant step in Hungary's space capabilities.

title: April/May Magazine: Space as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Via Satellite's April/May magazine focuses on space as critical infrastructure and cybersecurity challenges for lunar activities. It also covers Europe's ramp-up in space defense.
